perturbation.original_text: Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser further unde rstands and acknowledges that the Company does not make any representations or warrantie s as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials or any portion thereof. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. While the Company aims for accuracy, any errors or omissions will be promptly corrected upon discovery and notification by Potential Purchaser. Potential Purchaser further unde rstands and acknowledges that the Company does not make any representations or warrantie s as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials or any portion thereof. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modification inserts an in-text contradiction. The addition of 'While the Company aims for accuracy, any errors or omissions will be promptly corrected upon discovery and notification by Potential Purchaser', implies a duty to correct errors, directly conflicting with the disclaimer that 'the Company does not make any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials'. The original text disclaims responsibility for accuracy; the modified text suggests an obligation to fix inaccuracies when notified. This creates uncertainty about the Company's liability regarding the Evaluation Materials.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. While the Company aims for accuracy, any errors or omissions will be promptly corrected upon discovery and notification by Potential Purchaser. Potential Purchaser further unde rstands and acknowledges that the Company does not make any representations or warrantie s as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials or any portion thereof. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an obligation for the company to correct errors upon notification, which directly contradicts the subsequent statement that the company makes no warranties about the accuracy or completeness of the evaluation materials. The original text disclaims responsibility, while the changed text implies a duty to correct errors, thus creating a direct inconsistency within the same section of the document. This adheres to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Unless and until a definitive binding agreement is entered into between the Company and Potential Purchaser (or any of its affiliates) with respect to the Proposed Transaction, none of the parties hereto will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever to consummate, or otherwise with respect to, any Proposed Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to herein. Except for the matters set forth in this Agreement or in any such definitive binding agreement, neither pa rty hereto shall be entitled to rely on any statement, promise, agreement or understa nding, whether oral or written, any custom, usage of trade, course of dealing or conduct.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless and until a definitive binding agreement is entered into between the Company and Potential Purchaser (or any of its affiliates) with respect to the Proposed Transaction, none of the parties hereto will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever to consummate, or otherwise with respect to, any Proposed Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to herein. Notwithstanding the foregoing, both parties agree to negotiate in good faith towards a definitive agreement for a period of 30 days from the date of this agreement. Except for the matters set forth in this Agreement or in any such definitive binding agreement, neither pa rty hereto shall be entitled to rely on any statement, promise, agreement or understa nding, whether oral or written, any custom, usage of trade, course of dealing or conduct.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, both parties agree to negotiate in good faith towards a definitive agreement for a period of 30 days from the date of this agreement,' which creates an in-text contradiction with the preceding clause stating that neither party has any legal obligation to consummate the Proposed Transaction until a definitive agreement is signed. The 'good faith' negotiation clause implies an obligation to work towards an agreement, conflicting with the prior statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless and until a definitive binding agreement is entered into between the Company and Potential Purchaser (or any of its affiliates) with respect to the Proposed Transaction, none of the parties hereto will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever to consummate, or otherwise with respect to, any Proposed Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or any other written or oral expression, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to herein. Notwithstanding the foregoing, both parties agree to negotiate in good faith towards a definitive agreement for a period of 30 days from the date of this agreement. Except for the matters set forth in this Agreement or in any such definitive binding agreement, neither pa rty hereto shall be entitled to rely on any statement, promise, agreement or understa nding, whether oral or written, any custom, usage of trade, course of dealing or conduct.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that both parties agree to negotiate in good faith towards a definitive agreement for 30 days. This directly contradicts the preceding clause, and the contradicted text, which states that neither party is under any legal obligation to consummate the Proposed Transaction until a definitive agreement is signed. The good faith negotiation clause implies a legal obligation to work towards an agreement, creating a clear and direct conflict. As such, the change aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties regarding the subject matte r hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, arrangements and agreements, whether oral or in writing, between the parties relating in any way to such subject matter. The terms of this Agreement shall control over any additional purported confidentiality requirements imposed by an offering memorandum, web-base d database or similar repository of Evaluation Materials to which Potential Purc haser or its Representatives are granted access in connection with this Agreement, notwithstanding acceptance of such an offering memorandum or submission of an electronic signature, “clicking” on an “I Agree” icon or other indication of assent to such additional confidentiality conditions, it being understood and agreed that Potential Pu rchaser’s confidentiality obligations with respect to the Evaluation Materials are excl usively governed by this Agreement and may not be enlarged except by an agreement ex ecuted by the parties hereto in traditional written format.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties regarding the subject matte r hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, arrangements and agreements, whether oral or in writing, between the parties relating in any way to such subject matter. Except as otherwise explicitly agreed upon in writing and signed by both parties. The terms of this Agreement shall control over any additional purported confidentiality requirements imposed by an offering memorandum, web-base d database or similar repository of Evaluation Materials to which Potential Purc haser or its Representatives are granted access in connection with this Agreement, notwithstanding acceptance of such an offering memorandum or submission of an electronic signature, “clicking” on an “I Agree” icon or other indication of assent to such additional confidentiality conditions, it being understood and agreed that Potential Pu rchaser’s confidentiality obligations with respect to the Evaluation Materials are excl usively governed by this Agreement and may not be enlarged except by an agreement ex ecuted by the parties hereto in traditional written format.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation "Except as otherwise explicitly agreed upon in writing and signed by both parties." conflicts with the following statement: "Potential Purchaser’s confidentiality obligations with respect to the Evaluation Materials are excl usively governed by this Agreement and may not be enlarged except by an agreement ex ecuted by the parties hereto in traditional written format." This contradiction arises because the first insertion allows for changes to the entire agreement with a signed written document, while the second part specifically restricts the changes only to the confidentiality obligations via a traditionally written format, potentially leading to disputes over whether certain changes are valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties regarding the subject matte r hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, arrangements and agreements, whether oral or in writing, between the parties relating in any way to such subject matter. Except as otherwise explicitly agreed upon in writing and signed by both parties. The terms of this Agreement shall control over any additional purported confidentiality requirements imposed by an offering memorandum, web-base d database or similar repository of Evaluation Materials to which Potential Purc haser or its Representatives are granted access in connection with this Agreement, notwithstanding acceptance of such an offering memorandum or submission of an electronic signature, “clicking” on an “I Agree” icon or other indication of assent to such additional confidentiality conditions, it being understood and agreed that Potential Pu rchaser’s confidentiality obligations with respect to the Evaluation Materials are excl usively governed by this Agreement and may not be enlarged except by an agreement ex ecuted by the parties hereto in traditional written format.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces "Except as otherwise explicitly agreed upon in writing and signed by both parties." This statement broadens the scope of permissible modifications to the entire agreement, which contradicts the subsequent clause stating "Potential Purchaser’s confidentiality obligations with respect to the Evaluation Materials are exclusively governed by this Agreement and may not be enlarged except by an agreement executed by the parties hereto in traditional written format." The added exception now allows for the whole agreement to be changed with a signed written document, whereas before the confidentiality obligations could only be altered via traditional written format. Since the contradicted text is the whole paragraph again, it is clear that this inconsistency creates a strong contradiction within the document regarding how the agreement can be modified, aligning with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this agreement shall be the earlier of: (i) six (6) months from the date hereof; or (ii) the consummation of a Proposed Transac tion even if such Proposed Transaction is between the Company (or its Representatives) and a non-related third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this agreement shall be the earlier of: (i) six (6) months from the date hereof; (ii) twelve (12) months from the date hereof; or (iii) the consummation of a Proposed Transac tion even if such Proposed Transaction is between the Company (or its Representatives) and a non-related third party.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of '(ii) twelve (12) months from the date hereof;' introduces an in-text contradiction by having two different time periods after which the agreement can expire. Both (i) and (ii) contradict each other, making it unclear when the term of the agreement actually ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this agreement shall be the earlier of: (i) six (6) months from the date hereof; (ii) twelve (12) months from the date hereof; or (iii) the consummation of a Proposed Transac tion even if such Proposed Transaction is between the Company (or its Representatives) and a non-related third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states the agreement lasts for the earlier of six months or the consummation of a transaction. The changed text adds a second time period of twelve months, creating an in-text contradiction because the agreement could now expire at either six or twelve months, making the expiration date unclear, in the same location, as both sections are the same text.
